commit.message:
feat(linear): automate service request portal kanban setup (#22)

* feat(linear): automate backlog import and dependency linking

Summary:
- align the kanban docs and generated status suggestion to the existing
  elixir Linear workflow using Backlog as the intake state
- add a bundle importer that creates issues with project, milestone,
  label, priority, and state metadata from linear_import_rich.csv
- add a dependency linker that applies blocked-by relations from the
  generated dependency CSV using the import manifest

Rationale:
- the repo previously described a Triage/Ready-style workflow that did
  not match the actual Linear team states used by Symphony automation
- importing from the generated bundle needed a repeatable path that
  preserves milestone and label structure without manual setup drift
- blocker links should be reproducible from source data so the backlog
  can be rebuilt safely before execution kickoff

Tests:
- python3 project/linear/tools/build_linear_import_bundle.py
- python3 -m py_compile project/linear/tools/import_linear_bundle.py
  project/linear/tools/link_linear_dependencies.py
  project/linear/tools/upload_linear_backlog.py
  project/linear/tools/build_linear_import_bundle.py
- importer dry run and real import against Linear project
  Service Request Portal MVP (44 issues created)
- dependency linker dry run and real apply against the generated
  manifest (97 blocker relations created)

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

* fix(workflow): point tracker at service request portal project

Summary:
- update WORKFLOW.md frontmatter to use the new Service Request Portal
  MVP Linear project slug

Rationale:
- the workflow frontmatter still pointed at the old hello-world project
  and would target the wrong Linear project for unattended runs
- the imported kanban backlog now lives in the Service Request Portal
  MVP project and the tracker metadata must match it

Tests:
- not run (frontmatter-only metadata change)

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
fix(deps): override path-to-regexp to >=0.1.13 to resolve ReDoS CVE (#942)

CVE-2026-4867 (GHSA-37ch-88jc-xwx2): path-to-regexp < 0.1.13 is vulnerable
to Regular Expression Denial of Service via multiple route parameters.
The vulnerable version was pulled in transitively by express@4.22.1 via
@copilotkitnext/runtime. Override resolves all instances to 8.3.0.

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>
Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat(chatview): harden pi chat and no-focus desktop automation

Summary:
- extract chat model selection into a dedicated controller and
  centralize setup prompt helpers for cleaner chatview ownership
- preserve non-Pi transcript history across Pi sync, reset fresh-chat
  composer state correctly, and clean up duplicate embeddings status
  bars after reloads
- harden the desktop automation bridge, stable-client bootstrap,
  reload/chatview stress runners, and release preflight privacy and
  gitignore safety checks

Rationale:
- keep the Pi-integrated chatview simpler while fixing the real model
  switch, fresh-chat, and transcript continuity regressions exposed by
  live vault testing
- make no-focus Obsidian automation the canonical release-proof path,
  including reload churn, bridge recovery, and duplicate-status-bar
  detection
- block local-only, secret-like, or missing-ignore release hazards
  before version tagging and release publication

Tests:
- git diff --check
- git check-ignore -v .env.local .codex/diagnostics/check-plugin-fast-debug-20260224-015345.log .codex/diagnostics/check-plugin-fast-debug-20260224-015444.log .codex/diagnostics/check-plugin-fast-verbose-20260224-015427.log
- npm run check:plugin:fast
- npm run build
- npm test
- node --test testing/native/desktop-automation/bootstrap.test.mjs testing/native/desktop-automation/client.test.mjs testing/native/desktop-automation/runner.test.mjs
- npm run sync:local
- node scripts/reload-local-obsidian-plugin.mjs
- npm run test:native:desktop:chatview-stress
- npm run test:native:desktop:stress
- node scripts/release-plugin.mjs --dry-run --version 5.3.0
  --allow-dirty --notes-file docs/release-notes/5.3.0.md

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
perf(mobile-storefront): migrate FlatList to FlashList v2 (#940)

* fix(deps): override node-forge to 1.4.0 to resolve 4 high CVEs

Adds pnpm override for node-forge (via @unlighthouse/cli → listhen)
to fix signature forgery, DoS, and certificate bypass vulnerabilities.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(channels): restore jumia logo asset

* fix: address CodeRabbit review feedback on PR 939

- Move jumia-logo.png into src/assets and use @/ alias import
- Pin node-forge override to exact version 1.4.0

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* perf(mobile-storefront): migrate FlatList to FlashList v2 across 8 screens

Replace FlatList with @shopify/flash-list (v2) in all eligible list screens
for better virtualization and recycling performance. FlashList v2 auto-measures
items, so no estimatedItemSize needed. Also removes FlatList-specific perf
hacks (removeClippedSubviews, maxToRenderPerBatch, windowSize, getItemLayout,
columnWrapperStyle) that FlashList handles internally.

Migrated: category, search, orders, notifications, receipts, saved (tab + page),
SearchOverlay.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* perf(mobile): upgrade @shopify/flash-list 2.0.2 → 2.3.1 and migrate ChatModal

- Bump flash-list to 2.3.1 in both mobile-storefront and mobile-admin
- Migrate ChatModal from FlatList to FlashList (now possible with v2.3.1's
  inverted prop support and FlashListRef type)
- Update use-chat.ts ref type from FlatList to FlashListRef<ChatMessage>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

commit.message:
COE-312: Add session notes, outcome states, and artifact registry (#15)

* feat(session,artifact): add session notes, outcome states, and artifact registry

Summary:
- Add SessionOutcomeState enum (valid, invalid, aborted) to domain models
- Add notes and outcome_state fields to Session domain model and ORM
- Add experiment_id field to Artifact model for experiment-scoped artifacts
- Create database migration 520517cac40b for schema changes
- Update SessionService with outcome state handling and notes support
- Create ArtifactRegistryService for artifact management
- Add ArtifactRepository interface for artifact persistence
- Update session CLI with --notes option on create, --outcome on finalize
- Add session add-notes CLI command for updating notes
- Create artifact CLI commands: register, list, show, remove
- Link artifacts to sessions or experiments (at least one required)

Rationale:
- Operators need to document session context with notes during/after runs
- Outcome states allow marking sessions as invalid/aborted while keeping them
  visible for audit (they can be excluded from comparisons)
- Artifact registry enables attaching exports/reports to sessions or experiments
  for traceability and comparison workflows

Tests:
- make test: 77 tests passed
- make type-check: passed

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

* fix: address review feedback - remove duplicate SessionService, add comprehensive tests

- Remove duplicate SessionService from services_abc.py (keep services/session_service.py)
- Add tests for add-notes CLI command (3 tests)
- Add tests for artifact CLI commands (6 tests)
- Add tests for SQLArtifactRepository (7 tests)
- Add tests for update_session_notes service method (4 tests)
- Fix UUID slicing in artifact list command
- Add ArtifactRegistryService to exports

All 235 tests passing

* fix: remove ArtifactRegistryService due to architectural mismatch

- Removed ArtifactRegistryService from services_abc.py
- The service used domain models but repository expects ORM models
- This is a broader architectural pattern in the codebase
- CLI already uses SQLArtifactRepository directly with proper validation
- Updated exports in services/__init__.py

235 tests passing

* COE-312: Address PR feedback - restore --status parameter, add evidence

- Restore --status parameter to finalize command for backward compatibility
- Add --outcome parameter as optional (defaults to 'valid')
- Add test for --status parameter backward compatibility
- Create EVIDENCE_COE-312.md with CLI examples for all new features
- Fix linting issues with ruff --fix
- All 236 tests passing

* COE-312: Remove developer workflow scripts

- Remove scripts/create_workpad.py (Linear workflow helper)
- Remove scripts/linear_helper.py (Linear API helper)
- These were development tools not part of the benchmark system

---------

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
fix(mobile-storefront): fix Colors type import in product-card types (#941)

Use `import type Colors` with `(typeof Colors)['light']` to correctly
reference the color scheme type. The previous `import type { default as Colors }`
with `Colors['light']` caused TS2749 since TypeScript treated Colors as a value.

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>
Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
🎨 Palette: [UX improvement] Improve PhoneInput accessibility (#934)

* 🎨 Palette: [UX improvement] Improve PhoneInput accessibility

* What: Added proper accessibility attributes to the country selector modal and touchables in PhoneInput component.
* Why: Complex React Native custom form controls with decorative elements cause screen reader noise by individually reading them. Grouping elements inside TouchableOpacity with specific roles and hints improves UX.
* Accessibility:
  - Added accessibilityRole="button", accessibilityLabel, accessibilityHint, and activeOpacity={0.7} to the country selector button.
  - Added accessibilityRole="header" to the modal header text.
  - Hid decorative icons, flags, and duplicated text inside the country button and country row touchables using accessibilityElementsHidden={true} and importantForAccessibility="no".
  - Added accessibilityState={{ selected: true/false }} to country row options.
* Impact: Enhances accessibility for screen reader users selecting country codes within the mobile storefront app.

Co-authored-by: ogabasseyy <196925138+ogabasseyy@users.noreply.github.com>

* style(mobile-storefront): fix indentation in PhoneInput accessibility props

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Manuscript: sync field-scoping revisions and PDF

Co-Authored-By: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

fix(jumia): await refetch before URL replace on connect (#937)

* fix(jumia): await refetch before URL replace on successful connect

refetch() was called without await, then router.replace() fired
immediately — the re-render could interrupt the data fetch, leaving
the page showing stale (disconnected) state.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(jumia): use static import for logo to bypass [slug] route

The /jumia-logo.png public path was being caught by the [slug]
dynamic route on production, returning a 404 HTML page instead of
the image. Using a static import serves it from _next/static instead.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat(jumia): auto-sync orders for newly connected shops

After OAuth connect, the callback now includes shop IDs in the
redirect URL. The channels page reads these IDs and automatically
triggers order sync only for the newly connected shops, avoiding
unnecessary re-sync of pre-existing integrations.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(jumia): use public logo path in channels page

* fix(channels): address review — await refetch before replace, fix mock type

- Move router.replace inside refetch().then() to prevent stale state
- Filter shopIds to only active integrations in callback route
- Fix mockRefetch to resolve [] instead of undefined

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(jumia): scope auto-sync to newly connected shops

commit.message:
fix(mcp): tighten placeholder-backed auth headers

Summary:
- require sensitive MCP header values to be fully placeholder-backed instead of accepting any placeholder substring
- inspect env-expanded header values for vault placeholders so missing vault keys fail preflight with actionable auth_required details
- add regression tests for mixed raw-plus-placeholder headers and env-backed vault placeholders, and record the review follow-up in the trust-boundary docs

Rationale:
- PR review found that mixed raw secrets could bypass the new MCP credential-isolation boundary by appending a dummy placeholder
- env-backed vault placeholders were not being re-scanned after env substitution, which could degrade missing credentials into opaque downstream auth failures

Tests:
- python3 -m py_compile backend/src/api/mcp.py backend/src/tools/mcp_manager.py backend/tests/test_mcp_api.py backend/tests/test_mcp_manager.py
- cd backend && .venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_mcp_api.py tests/test_mcp_manager.py tests/test_tools_api.py -q
- cd backend && .venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_eval_harness.py::test_runtime_eval_scenarios_expose_expected_details tests/test_eval_harness.py::test_main_lists_available_scenarios -q
- git diff --check
- cd docs && npm run build

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
fix(identity): address review feedback after rebase

Co-Authored-By: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

feat(migrations): stream import job progress via Supabase Realtime (#935)

* fix(jumia): relax token schema to match Vendor Center API response

The JumiaTokenResponseSchema was too strict — it required exact `Bearer`/`N_A`
enum for token_type and strict number types for expires_in fields. Jumia's
Vendor Center API may return lowercase token_type or string-encoded numbers,
causing Zod validation to reject valid token responses during OAuth callback.

Also improves error logging in the callback catch-all to include structured
error details instead of raw error object.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(jumia): harden token schema and callback error logging

- Replace z.coerce.number() with union(number, numeric-string) to reject
  booleans/arrays that coerce silently
- Add .trim() to all string validators to reject whitespace-only values
- Normalize non-Error throwables in callback catch-all to a safe shape
  instead of logging raw thrown objects
- Add negative-path tests: empty/null token_type, non-numeric strings,
  boolean/array expires_in

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat(jumia): add request throttling to respect Vendor Center rate limits

Jumia enforces 200 req/min and 4 req/sec at MasterShop level. Without
throttling, paginated getAll*() calls burst through this limit during
product imports or order syncs for busy merchants.

- Add 250ms minimum interval between requests in JumiaClient.request()
- Add 250ms delay between pages in edge function order sync loop
- Add throttle test verifying sequential requests are spaced correctly

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(jumia): add diagnostic error codes to OAuth callback for root cause analysis

Token exchange failures now return `?error=token_exchange_failed` instead of
generic `connection_failed`, with detailed logging of the redirect URI and
HTTP status. Also adds user-friendly error messages for the new codes and
aligns connect route to use `getAppUrl()` for redirect URI consistency.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(jumia): normalize oauth callback redirect uri

* fix(jumia): serialize request throttling

* fix(jumia): tighten request gate release handling

* fix(jumia): address oauth review findings

* fix(jumia): tighten callback and refresh handling

* fix(jumia): harden callback config and client throttling

* fix(deps): override brace-expansion and happy-dom to resolve security alerts

- brace-expansion ^5.0.2 → ^5.0.5 (DoS via zero-step sequence)
- happy-dom >=20.8.8 (unsanitized export names → code execution)

Resolves all 6 open Dependabot alerts.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(jumia): guard oauth redirects with app url

* fix(jumia): require explicit oauth redirect uri

* fix(jumia): log token exchange diagnostics

* fix(jumia): harden error detail sanitization

* feat(migrations): stream import job progress via Supabase Realtime

Replace 1s polling with Supabase Realtime subscription on import_jobs
for instant progress updates, with a 5s polling fallback for resilience.

- Add adaptive progress reporter cadence (~20 writes/job vs fixed 25-row)
- Enable Realtime on import_jobs table (idempotent migration)
- Show indeterminate progress bar during initial data loading phase
- Handle terminal transitions (validating → preview_ready) with row fetch
- Invalidate in-flight polls when Realtime updates arrive
- Add reduced-motion support for progress animation

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(migrations): address CodeRabbit review findings

- Add background: true to terminal transition refreshJob call
- Handle Realtime subscribe errors (CHANNEL_ERROR/TIMED_OUT)
- Assert aria-valuenow absent for indeterminate progress
- Tighten adaptive write count bounds (20-27 instead of 10-35)
- Add tests for subscribe error handling and background flag

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat(migrations): wire Realtime callbacks into migration jobs hook

Connect onRealtimeJobUpdate and onRefreshRequestIdBump from
useMigrationJobs into useMigrationJobPolling so Realtime payloads
merge into the jobs list via mergeJobs and invalidate stale polls.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(migrations): address stale ref, aria-valuenow, test perf, and migration guard

- Terminal transition uses includeJob: true so refreshJob reads the fresh
  server status instead of stale selectedJobRef (race condition fix)
- Forward value prop to ProgressPrimitive.Root so Radix sets aria-valuenow
  on determinate progress bars and omits it for indeterminate
- Replace 5823-row iteration with boundary-based calls in adaptive step
  test (exact 26 writes assertion, much faster)
- Guard migration against missing supabase_realtime publication so it's
  safe in environments without Realtime

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(tests): use exact counts and monotonic sequences in progress reporter tests

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix(virtual-camera): stabilize demand lifecycle and release flow

Summary:
- gate host preview on real capture demand so the menubar app stays idle
  without an open window or external virtual camera consumer
- move early wake-up to authorizedToStartStream, add demand inspection and
  Chromium/native probe harnesses, and harden the background test scripts
- add local install and DMG publishing skills, bump the app and sysext to
  0.1.0 (10), and refresh the manual release asset workflow

Rationale:
- the host was keeping the physical camera alive after UI teardown because
  preview startup and shutdown were still coupled to appearance state
- the sysext wake-up path was too noisy and a stale extension process hid
  the real behavior until the bundle version forced a clean restart
- reproducible local probes and a repeatable DMG flow are needed so the
  fix can be validated and shipped without manual QA loops

Tests:
- xcodebuild -project Reframe.xcodeproj -scheme Reframe -destination
  'platform=macOS' -derivedDataPath build/test test
- ./.agents/skills/local-test/scripts/build_and_install.sh
- build/cli/Build/Products/Release/reframe print-demand
- CLI_PATH="$PWD/build/cli/Build/Products/Release/reframe" APP_PATH='/Applications/Reframe.app' ./Scripts/run-chromium-background-virtual-camera-probe.sh
- ./.agents/skills/update-dmg/scripts/build_release_dmg.sh /Applications/Reframe.app build/Reframe-0.1.0.dmg
- ./.agents/skills/update-dmg/scripts/publish_release_asset.sh v0.1.0 build/Reframe-0.1.0.dmg

commit.message:
Add standard open source project metadata

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

refactor: separate file stream aborts from fusillade errors (#183)

## Summary
- add `FileStreamItem::Abort` and `FileStreamResult` so
producer-initiated aborts are no longer surfaced as fusillade validation
errors
- update `create_file_stream` to return `Result<FileStreamResult>` and
roll back cleanly on abort
- deprecate `FileStreamItem::Error(String)` while preserving the old
path for compatibility, and update tests to cover abort behavior

## Why
Producer-side aborts are not owned by fusillade. Treating them as
`Err(FusilladeError::ValidationError(String))` forced callers to
round-trip their own failures through fusillade, erased type
information, and encouraged fragile string matching. This change
separates producer intent (`Abort`) from actual fusillade failures and
makes the ownership boundary explicit.

## Context
Fixes
https://linear.app/doubleword/issue/COR-14/clean-abort-signaling-in-fusillade

## Local verification
- `cargo fmt --check`
- `SQLX_OFFLINE=true cargo check --lib`
- `SQLX_OFFLINE=true cargo clippy --lib -- -D warnings`

I also attempted to run the DB-backed CI path locally, including
starting PostgreSQL with the system binaries and Debian cluster tooling,
but this sandbox blocks the required privilege transition to the
`postgres` user, so full local `cargo test` / `just ci` could not be
completed here.

---------

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
fix: improve context overflow detection and condenser handling (#63)

* fix(openhands): improve context overflow detection and condenser handling

Summary:
- Extract error details from conversation state for better error detection
- Reset conversations when workflow now has condenser but existing doesn't
- Start local server for CLI rehydrate command when needed

Rationale:
- Error detection was only capturing "error" status, not actual error message
- Old conversations without condenser should be reset to get condenser benefits
- CLI rehydrate command now works without a pre-running server

Tests:
- cargo test --package opensymphony-openhands --package opensymphony-cli (all pass)
- cargo clippy --package opensymphony-openhands --package opensymphony-cli (clean)

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

* fix(openhands): include conversation stats in state mirror for token tracking

Summary:
- Merge conversation.stats into raw_state when applying conversation to state mirror
- This fixes token tracking showing 0 because stats.usage_to_metrics.default.accumulated_token_usage was not available

Rationale:
- The accumulated_token_usage() function reads from stats.usage_to_metrics.default.accumulated_token_usage
- OpenHands provides this data in the Conversation.stats field from the REST API
- Without merging stats into raw_state, token tracking would always return None

Tests:
- cargo test --package opensymphony-openhands (all pass)
- Added state_mirror_includes_stats_from_conversation test

commit.message:
fix(jumia): make refresh_token optional in token schema (#933)

* fix(jumia): relax token schema to match Vendor Center API response

The JumiaTokenResponseSchema was too strict — it required exact `Bearer`/`N_A`
enum for token_type and strict number types for expires_in fields. Jumia's
Vendor Center API may return lowercase token_type or string-encoded numbers,
causing Zod validation to reject valid token responses during OAuth callback.

Also improves error logging in the callback catch-all to include structured
error details instead of raw error object.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(jumia): harden token schema and callback error logging

- Replace z.coerce.number() with union(number, numeric-string) to reject
  booleans/arrays that coerce silently
- Add .trim() to all string validators to reject whitespace-only values
- Normalize non-Error throwables in callback catch-all to a safe shape
  instead of logging raw thrown objects
- Add negative-path tests: empty/null token_type, non-numeric strings,
  boolean/array expires_in

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat(jumia): add request throttling to respect Vendor Center rate limits

Jumia enforces 200 req/min and 4 req/sec at MasterShop level. Without
throttling, paginated getAll*() calls burst through this limit during
product imports or order syncs for busy merchants.

- Add 250ms minimum interval between requests in JumiaClient.request()
- Add 250ms delay between pages in edge function order sync loop
- Add throttle test verifying sequential requests are spaced correctly

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(jumia): add diagnostic error codes to OAuth callback for root cause analysis

Token exchange failures now return `?error=token_exchange_failed` instead of
generic `connection_failed`, with detailed logging of the redirect URI and
HTTP status. Also adds user-friendly error messages for the new codes and
aligns connect route to use `getAppUrl()` for redirect URI consistency.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(jumia): normalize oauth callback redirect uri

* fix(jumia): serialize request throttling

* fix(jumia): tighten request gate release handling

* fix(jumia): address oauth review findings

* fix(jumia): tighten callback and refresh handling

* fix(jumia): harden callback config and client throttling

* fix(deps): override brace-expansion and happy-dom to resolve security alerts

- brace-expansion ^5.0.2 → ^5.0.5 (DoS via zero-step sequence)
- happy-dom >=20.8.8 (unsanitized export names → code execution)

Resolves all 6 open Dependabot alerts.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(jumia): guard oauth redirects with app url

* fix(jumia): require explicit oauth redirect uri

* fix(jumia): log token exchange diagnostics

* fix(jumia): harden error detail sanitization

* fix(jumia): make refresh_token optional in token schema

Jumia's /token endpoint does not always return refresh_token or
refresh_expires_in. Making these fields optional unblocks the OAuth
flow that was failing with a Zod validation error (502).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(jumia): validate refresh token before attempting refresh

Throws a clear 401 error when refresh_token is missing/empty instead
of sending an empty string to Jumia's /token endpoint.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix cursor overshoot by serializing SSH input writes

Replace frontend ACK-based input queue with backend serialization.
Each SSH session now has a dedicated write task that processes input
sequentially, eliminating concurrent channel writes that scrambled
input order. Frontend sends fire-and-forget, backend guarantees ordering.

Fixes intermittent cursor position errors during rapid arrow key edits.

commit.message:
fix: enable condenser by default and auto-recover from context overflow (#62)

* fix: enable condenser by default and auto-recover from context overflow

The OpenHands built-in condensation mechanism was not enabled by default,
allowing conversations to exceed the model's maximum context length
(262,143 tokens). This caused ConversationRunError failures that required
manual rehydration.

Changes:
- Enable LLMSummarizingCondenser by default (max_size=240, keep_first=2)
  when the condenser block is omitted from WORKFLOW.md
- Add context overflow error detection (prompt-too-long patterns)
- Auto-recover from context overflow: close old stream, rehydrate to fresh
  conversation with token preservation, retry the turn
- Fix rehydrate_conversation() to return RehydrationFailed with actual
  cause instead of opaque UnexpectedEarlyResult
- Update tests and documentation for new condenser default

* refactor(openhands): extract turn execution and add retry guard

Summary:
- Extract turn execution into execute_turn() helper method
- Extract recovery logic into recover_from_context_overflow() helper
- Add MAX_CONTEXT_OVERFLOW_RETRIES=1 guard to prevent infinite loops
- Reduce nesting from 4+ levels to 2 levels in run_with_observer()

Rationale:
- Addresses PR review feedback on code complexity and infinite retry risk
- Helper methods eliminate code duplication of turn execution flow
- Retry guard ensures at most one recovery attempt per session

Tests:
- cargo test --package opensymphony-openhands (85 passed)
- cargo clippy --package opensymphony-openhands (clean)

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

---------

commit.message:
fix: 削除モーダルを制御前提に揃える

DeletePaymentModal の非制御利用前提を削除し、open と onClose を必須にした。\nStory も制御前提に揃えてレビュー指摘に対応した。

Co-Authored-By: Codex <codex@openai.com>

fix: MSW の削除レスポンスを簡素化する

payments の MSW ハンドラを成功レスポンス中心の実装に寄せた。\n削除失敗 Story の loader では handler を reset してから override するようにした。

Co-Authored-By: Codex <codex@openai.com>

fix(evals): Stabilize workflow behavior and harness auth (#127)

Stabilize the eval workflow paths that were still failing or proving
brittle. OAuth callback resume now reads persisted thread state
directly, subscribed follow-up routing has clearer guidance for genuine
Junior follow-ups, and the GitHub skill now keeps duplicate checks
silent in the final reply.

This also hardens the harness itself. Vitest env loading now preserves
caller-provided env vars while still letting later .env files override
earlier ones, and eval log capture is scoped to the `slackEval` task so
timed-out scenarios cannot leak gateway-auth failures into later evals.

The branch also adds `pnpm cloudflare:token`, which regenerates the
named Cloudflare tunnel token with `cloudflared` and upserts it into the
root `.env.local`. That makes the stable local Slack tunnel workflow
repeatable after `vercel env pull` rewrites `.env.local`.

Added route-level integration coverage for generic OAuth and MCP OAuth
resume, plus focused unit coverage for env precedence and the eval
harness behavior seam. Locally this passes the targeted OAuth resume
suites, subscribed-message routing suites, env loader tests, eval
harness unit tests, `pnpm --filter @sentry/junior exec tsc --noEmit`,
and a real `pnpm cloudflare:token` refresh for the default `junior-dev`
tunnel. The remaining GitHub skill eval should be rerun outside Codex
against this branch.

---------

feat(trust): harden privileged execution boundaries

Summary:
- move manual MCP credential updates onto vault-backed placeholders and reject raw sensitive headers in MCP config flows
- bind workflow approvals and replay/resume to approval-context snapshots instead of stale tool-plus-argument assumptions
- split vault operations onto a dedicated delegation specialist and pin the secret-routing boundary with runtime eval coverage

Rationale:
- keep connector auth, approval recovery, and delegated execution on explicit trust surfaces instead of reusing stale or overly broad paths
- prevent generic planning or memory delegation from inheriting direct secret-management capability
